type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both our features need to touch the same two files (Cache class + configure_cache signature), so a clean file-level split isn't possible. Proposal: timeslice instead of partition. My TTL changes are small and additive (new ttl param on Cache.__init__/configure_cache, a set_ttl() helper, one __all__ entry, TTLCache import, expire() call in get/put). I'll make them now and send DONE within a few minutes. Please hold off editing cache.py/__init__.py until you see my DONE, then re-read the files and layer your compression/eviction-knob changes on top (also additive params, so should merge cleanly). Sound OK?

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished TTL feature. Changes: cache.py - added ttl param to Cache.__init__, TTLCache import/usage for memory layer, disk_cache.set(expire=ttl) in put(), disk_cache.expire() lazy purge on get() miss. __init__.py - added ttl param to configure_cache(), new set_ttl(seconds) helper, ttl=None on DSPY_CACHE, and 'set_ttl' added to __all__. All changes are additive (new optional trailing params / new function), tests pass (tests/clients/test_cache.py, tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py). You're clear to layer your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params on top now - just add them after my 'ttl' param in both Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures to avoid reordering positional args.
